subject: Science
chapter: The Amazing World of Solutes, Solvents, and Solutions
source_pdf: The Amazing World of Solutes Solvents and Solutions.pdf

activities_projects.title: Dissolving Salt in Water Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of solute, solvent, and solution
steps: Fill a clean glass tumbler halfway with water
Add one spoon of salt and stir until it dissolves
Keep adding salt gradually while stirring
Observe the stage when salt stops dissolving
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how a solute dissolves in a solvent to form a solution
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation table and explanation of dissolving process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Identifying Saturated and Unsaturated Solutions
activities_projects.objective: Understand the difference between saturated and unsaturated solutions
steps: Prepare a salt solution in water
Continue adding salt while stirring
Observe when undissolved salt settles at the bottom
Identify whether the solution is saturated or unsaturated
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the concept of saturation in solutions

activities_projects.title: Effect of Temperature on Solubility
activities_projects.objective: Study how temperature affects the solubility of solids
steps: Take water in a beaker and measure its temperature
Add baking soda and stir until dissolved
Heat the solution and continue stirring
Observe how more baking soda dissolves at higher temperature
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn that solubility of most solids increases with temperature

activities_projects.title: Measuring Mass Using Digital Balance
activities_projects.objective: Learn how to measure mass using a digital weighing balance
steps: Switch on the digital weighing balance
Ensure the reading is zero
Place a watch glass on the balance and tare it
Place a stone on the watch glass
Note the mass displayed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how mass of objects is measured accurately

activities_projects.title: Observing Measuring Cylinder Divisions
activities_projects.objective: Understand how to read the smallest measurement in a measuring cylinder
steps: Observe the maximum volume marked on the cylinder
Note the difference between two large marks
Count the number of small divisions
Calculate the smallest measurable volume
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how to measure liquid volume accurately
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct calculation of smallest division (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Measuring 50 mL of Water
activities_projects.objective: Practice accurate measurement of liquid volume
steps: Place a measuring cylinder on a flat surface
Pour water slowly into the cylinder
Adjust the level to 50 mL using a dropper
Read the measurement at the bottom of the meniscus
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn correct techniques for reading liquid levels
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in measuring liquid volume (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Calculating Volume of Regular Objects
steps: Measure length, width, and height of the object
Use the formula Volume = l × w × h
Calculate the volume
Record results in notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how to calculate volume of regular solids

activities_projects.title: Measuring Volume of Irregular Objects
activities_projects.objective: Determine the volume of irregular solids using displacement method
steps: Fill measuring cylinder with water and note initial volume
Tie a stone with thread
Lower the stone into the cylinder
Note the final water level
Calculate volume by subtracting initial volume from final volume

activities_projects.title: Calculating Density of a Solid
activities_projects.objective: Learn to calculate density using mass and volume
steps: Measure mass of the object using weighing balance
Measure volume using displacement method
Use formula Density = Mass / Volume
Calculate the density
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand density and its calculation

activities_projects.title: Floating Egg Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Understand density and buoyancy in liquids
steps: Fill a glass with water
Place a raw egg in the water and observe
Add salt gradually and stir
Observe the egg rising and floating
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how density of liquid affects floating objects
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of egg floating due to increased density (10 marks)
